# KazeUI

> A minimal, composable design system for React. Copy-paste components with clean defaults, sharp details, dark mode, and zero runtime dependencies.

## Overview

KazeUI is a component library where you own the code. Instead of installing a package, you use the CLI to copy component source files directly into your project. No npm dependency, no version conflicts, no runtime overhead.

- Website: https://kazeui.vercel.app
- GitHub: https://github.com/abderrahimghazali/kaze
- CLI: `npx kazeui-cli init` then `npx kazeui-cli add <component>`

## Stack

- React 19
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS v4
- Vite
- TanStack Router (docs site)
- CSS custom properties for theming (`--kaze-*` tokens)

## Components (19)

- Accordion: animated expand/collapse with height transitions
- Avatar: initials, images, status dots, ring accents, animated entrance
- AvatarGroup: stacked avatars with overflow count and hover fan-out
- Badge: six semantic variants (default, success, destructive, info, warning, outline)
- Button: five variants (primary, secondary, outline, ghost, destructive), three sizes, icon support
- ButtonGroup: joined button strip
- Card: surface container with hover elevation
- Checkbox: animated checkmark with label and disabled state
- CodeBlock: syntax-highlighted code display with line numbers and copy button
- DataTable: interactive table with search, column sorting, pagination, row selection
- Dialog: portaled modal overlay with scale-in animation
- Dropdown: click-triggered popover menu with items, icons, separators
- Input: text input with focus rings, icon slot, disabled state
- RadioGroup: single-select radio buttons with animated dot indicator
- Select: custom dropdown with single and multiple selection, check indicator
- Separator: horizontal divider with optional centered label
- Skeleton: shimmer loading placeholder with configurable shapes
- Table: data table with typed columns, row hover, striped option
- Tabs: underline tabs with sliding indicator animation
- Toast: auto-dismissing notifications with 5 variants via global `toast()` function
- Toggle: switch with thumb animation and ARIA roles

## Blocks (20)

Pre-built page sections combining multiple components:

- Hero (Aurora): animated gradient blobs + rotating typewriter headline
- CTA (Spotlight): orbiting radial light + grain texture on dark background
- 3D Card Effect: mouse-tracked perspective tilt with depth layers and glare
- Orbiting Icons: 3D atomic orbital paths with real brand logos
- Bento Grid: asymmetric card layout with mixed column/row spans
- Pricing: three-tier cards with feature lists and popular highlight
- Feature Grid: illustrated bento with chat scene, code editor, theme preview
- Logo Cloud: infinite-scrolling marquee with fade edges
- Stats: animated counters with shared-border grid
- Testimonials: infinite dual-column scroll at different speeds
- Terminal: typing animation with blinking cursor and replay
- Before/After: draggable slider comparing unstyled vs kaze-styled UI
- Magnetic Dock: macOS-style proximity magnification with brand logos
- Layer Stack: 3D exploded view of UI layers on hover
- Component Morph: smooth transitions between Button/Input/Badge/Card states
- Spotlight Cursor: dark overlay with flashlight cursor revealing hidden content
- Navbar: logo, links, and action buttons
- Footer: multi-column with link groups
- Login: centered form with social auth
- Sign Up: registration form with name grid

## Theming

All tokens are CSS custom properties. Toggle dark mode with:
```
document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', 'dark')
```

Light mode uses warm stone grays. Dark mode uses neutral black (#0A0A0A).

Key tokens: `--kaze-bg`, `--kaze-surface`, `--kaze-border`, `--kaze-text`, `--kaze-accent`, `--kaze-radius-sm/md/lg`, `--kaze-shadow-sm/md/lg`, `--kaze-transition`.

## CLI Usage

```bash
npx kazeui-cli init        # setup config + base files
npx kazeui-cli add button  # add a component
npx kazeui-cli list        # show all available components
```

## Documentation

- Introduction: https://kazeui.vercel.app/
- Theme Switcher: https://kazeui.vercel.app/theme
- Components: https://kazeui.vercel.app/components/{name}
- Blocks: https://kazeui.vercel.app/blocks/{name}
